questions.question: How many organs are annotated in the MICA dataset?
evidence_snippets: Each scan includes annotations for 11 organs.

questions.question: What type of model is the Full-CT?
questions.gold_answer: A pre-trained SegResNet model for volumetric (3D) segmentation.
evidence_snippets: The Full-CT model utilizes a pre-trained SegResNet model for volumetric (3D) segmentation.

questions.question: Which model, DeepEdit or Full-CT, had better segmentation performance, particularly for the organs sharing common labels?
evidence_snippets: Full-CT, pre-trained on human CT scans, surpasses DeepEdit in organs sharing common labels.

questions.question: How many training sessions were executed for each CNN model?
evidence_snippets: For each CNN model, we executed multiple training sessions.

questions.question: Why did DeepEdit sometimes predict zero volumes for certain organs?
questions.gold_answer: Because of small physical volumes and inconsistencies in segmentation masks.
evidence_snippets: prediction is extremely poor when: An organ has a small physical volume... There is an inconsistency in segmentation masks...

questions.question: Based on the analysis, what is a key limitation of the DeepEdit model compared to Full-CT?
questions.gold_answer: DeepEdit is trained on only 5 NHP CT data, while Full-CT is trained on 1228 human CT scans.
evidence_snippets: DeepEdit is trained on only 5 NHP CT data. The Full-CT model is trained on 1228 human CT scans.

questions.question: Refer to Table 1. How many CT scans are available in the MICA dataset?
What does Figure 2 illustrate concerning organ volume measurements in the MICA dataset?
questions.gold_answer: Lung volumes exhibit the highest maximum measurements, while right inguinal lymph node volumes are at the lower end.
evidence_snippets: Figure 2 displays the distribution of volume measurements... lung volumes exhibit the highest maximum measurements... right inguinal lymph node volumes occupy the lower end.

questions.question: What is one reason the study emphasized the need for diversity in training data?
questions.gold_answer: To effectively learn parameters and accurately predict outcomes based on the diversity of organs and their volumes.
evidence_snippets: A crucial need for additional training data arises... It is important for a model to encounter various scenarios related to a given organ.

questions.question: How many NHP IDs are listed in Table 2 of the report?
evidence_snippets: Table 2: IDs associated with NHPs in MICA datasets analyzed in this work.
